Calculated Hohmann transfer Delta-V from 700 km circular to 80 km perigee is ~172 m/s, strictly falling within this maximum tolerance limit allowing for targeting non-idealities.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies entirely on a synthetically generated specification document detailing an end-of-life disposal maneuver for the Aqua-Terra-2 spacecraft. The target coordinates for 'Point Nemo' are correctly enforced as latitude [-55.0, -35.0] degrees and longitude [220.0, 250.0] degrees East (or [-140.0, -110.0] degrees West). The reentry altitude interface is set at exactly 80.0 km. An independent mathematical check verifies that the required delta-V to lower a 700 km circular orbit's perigee to 80 km is ~172 m/s, which validates the acceptance range of [150.0, 250.0] m/s.
